mitigations.mitigation_name: Harden the Ubuntu host’s DNS client settings so it uses only the sanctioned recursive resolver and cannot fall back to alternate name servers

args.command:
cat /etc/resolv.conf && printf '
---
' && ls -l /etc/resolv.conf && printf '
---
' && readlink -f /etc/resolv.conf || true

printf 'nameserver 192.168.1.1
options timeout:1 attempts:1
' > /etc/resolv.conf && chmod 644 /etc/resolv.conf && ls -l /etc/resolv.conf && printf '
---
' && cat /etc/resolv.conf
ls -l /usr/bin/dig /usr/bin/nslookup /usr/bin/host /usr/bin/python3 /usr/bin/python3.12 && ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2 && wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; echo HTTP:$?

iptables -C OUTPUT -p udp -d 203.0.113.1 --dport 8853 -j REJECT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p udp -d 203.0.113.1 --dport 8853 -j REJECT; iptables -C OUTPUT -p tcp -d 203.0.113.1 --dport 8853 -j REJECT 2>/dev/null || iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p tcp -d 203.0.113.1 --dport 8853 -j REJECT; iptables -S OUTPUT | head -n 20

ping -c 1 -W 2 192.168.1.2; wget -q -T 4 --tries=1 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1; echo HTTP:$?; python3 - <<'PY'
import socket
s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
s.settimeout(1)
try:
    s.sendto(b'test', ('203.0.113.1', 8853))
    print('UDP_SEND_OK')
except Exception as e:
    print('UDP_SEND_ERR', e)
PY
